summary: Per-artifact budget pass is safe to ship: byte-measured strings are the exact bytes written, gates read full in-memory artifacts (verdicts unchanged), and every failure/within-budget path degrades to full-artifact references without dangling

findings.detail: buildBoundedArtifactHandoff sets full_artifact_path to the bare filename while read_path is workspace-relative; cosmetic inconsistency in advisory reviewer handoff text, not a dangling reference

stale-summary cleanup only iterates scannedArtifactFilenames, so when writeGateDagDump toggles true->false a pre-existing gate-dag.summary.json is left as a harmless orphan not referenced by the fresh artifact_budget report or handoff

inspection.summary: Read pr-artifact-budget.js, the pr-manager.js budget wiring (measure/write/cleanup and pr-body handoff), agent-review.js and canonical-audit.js handoff/ref changes, workspace.js config default, and the PAB test suite; confirmed byte-measured strings equal written bytes, gate verdicts unaffected, failure paths degrade to full paths, cleanup only removes known .summary.json siblings; full suite 851/851 pass per coordinator log

judgment_delta: Initial concern that budget measurement could diverge from written files, drift gate verdicts, or dangle handoff references -> pass because the same serialized strings are measured and written, gateStatus is computed independent of the budget pass with verdict identity asserted (PAB-S-7), and every failure branch resolves to the always-written full artifact; residuals are low-severity cosmetic/orphan-file only
